You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_20.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 20: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[20]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_20.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 20: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

7046	
7047	# A12 (iarch006 epic-failure): intermediate DATA-ONLY checkpoints for the two genuinely missing
7048	# stages — post-generation and post-verification. The post-fetch (fetch_snapshot.json, #1259) and
7049	# post-selection (corpus_snapshot.json, F04) checkpoints already exist; these two fill the gap so a
7050	# judge-only re-run is a cents-cost replay instead of a fresh ~$2.79 run.
7051	#
7052	# HARD INVARIANT (CLAUDE.md §-1.3, ABSOLUTE — mirrors corpus_snapshot.py): a checkpoint stores DATA
7053	# ONLY — raw drafts, evidence/model/env hashes, per-sentence verification DATA. It stores NO
7054	# faithfulness verdict, NO release_outcome, NO "verified"/"released" flag, NO D8 decision. A resume
7055	# MUST re-run every gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / D8) on the reloaded data — it can NEVER
7056	# replay a stored release_outcome. Writing a verdict here would be a RELAXATION of the only hard gate
7057	# and is an auto-P0; this helper makes that impossible by never accepting/serializing a verdict.
7058	_A12_POSTGEN_CHECKPOINT = "postgen_checkpoint.json"
7059	_A12_POSTVERIFY_CHECKPOINT = "postverify_checkpoint.json"
7060	
7061	
7062	def _a12_hash(obj: "Any") -> str:
7063	    """Stable SHA256 over a JSON-serializable object (sorted keys). Identity fingerprint only."""
7064	    try:
7065	        _blob = json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, default=str, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
7066	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a fingerprint must never abort a checkpoint
7067	        _blob = repr(obj).encode("utf-8", "replace")
7068	    return hashlib.sha256(_blob).hexdigest()
7069	
7070	
7071	def write_postgen_checkpoint(
7072	    run_dir: "Path",
7073	    *,
7074	    run_id: str,
7075	    question: str,
7076	    raw_drafts: "dict[str, str]",
7077	    evidence_ids: "list[str]",
7078	    model_pin: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
7079	    env_slate: "dict[str, str] | None" = None,
7080	) -> "Path | None":
7081	    """A12 post-generation checkpoint (DATA ONLY). Stores the raw section drafts + identity hashes so
7082	    a resume can re-enter BEFORE verification. Stores NO verdict. Best-effort: a write failure must
7083	    NOT abort the paid run. Returns the path written (or None on failure)."""
7084	    try:
7085	        payload = {
7086	            "stage": "post_generation",
7087	            "schema_version": 1,
7088	            "run_id": run_id,
7089	            "question": question,
7090	            "raw_drafts": dict(raw_drafts or {}),
7091	            "evidence_ids": list(evidence_ids or []),
7092	            "evidence_id_hash": _a12_hash(sorted(evidence_ids or [])),
7093	            "model_pin": dict(model_pin or {}),
7094	            "env_slate": dict(env_slate or {}),
7095	            # EXPLICIT invariant marker so a §-1.1 auditor sees the contract on the artifact itself.
7096	            "faithfulness_invariant": (
7097	                "DATA ONLY; no verdict/release_outcome stored; a resume MUST re-run every gate."
7098	            ),
7099	        }
7100	        _path = Path(run_dir) / _A12_POSTGEN_CHECKPOINT
7101	        _path.write_text(
7102	            json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
7103	        )
7104	        return _path
7105	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint is best-effort durability, never a run blocker
7106	        return None
7107	
7108	
7109	def write_postverify_checkpoint(
7110	    run_dir: "Path",
7111	    *,
7112	    run_id: str,
7113	    question: str,
7114	    verif_details: "dict[str, Any]",
7115	    evidence_ids: "list[str]",
7116	) -> "Path | None":
7117	    """A12 post-verification checkpoint (DATA ONLY). Stores the per-sentence verification DATA
7118	    (verif_details) so a resume can re-enter RIGHT BEFORE the judge seam and re-run only the
7119	    cents-cost judge. This is per-sentence ACCOUNTING DATA, NOT a verdict — the report is already
7120	    strict_verify-assembled on disk; a resume STILL re-runs the 4-role/D8 judge from scratch (no
7121	    stored release_outcome). Best-effort. Returns the path written (or None on failure)."""
7122	    try:
7123	        payload = {
7124	            "stage": "post_verification",
7125	            "schema_version": 1,
7126	            "run_id": run_id,
7127	            "question": question,
7128	            # verif_details is per-sentence kept/dropped ACCOUNTING (which sentence, which tokens,
7129	            # which drop-reason) — NOT a release verdict. It mirrors verification_details.json.
7130	            "verification_details": verif_details,
7131	            "evidence_id_hash": _a12_hash(sorted(evidence_ids or [])),
7132	            "faithfulness_invariant": (
7133	                "DATA ONLY; no D8/release verdict stored; a resume MUST re-run the 4-role/D8 judge."
7134	            ),
7135	        }
7136	        _path = Path(run_dir) / _A12_POSTVERIFY_CHECKPOINT
7137	        _path.write_text(
7138	            json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
7139	        )
7140	        return _path
7141	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint is best-effort durability, never a run blocker
7142	        return None
7143	
7144	
7145	# A12 (iarch007 P1): the verdict tokens a DATA-ONLY checkpoint may NEVER contain. A resume
7146	# re-runs every gate from the reloaded DATA — it can never replay a stored decision (§-1.3 ABSOLUTE).
7147	# If any of these keys appear at the TOP LEVEL of a checkpoint payload, a verdict leaked in and the
7148	# loader FAILS LOUD (a silent load would relax the only hard gate).
7149	_A12_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS = frozenset(
7150	    {
7151	        "release_outcome",
7152	        "release_allowed",
7153	        "released",
7154	        "verified",
7155	        "is_verified",
7156	        "final_verdicts",
7157	        "d8_decision",
7158	        "four_role_evaluation",
7159	    }
7160	)
7161	
7162	
7163	def load_a12_checkpoint(run_dir: "Path", checkpoint_name: str) -> "dict | None":
7164	    """A12 RESUME LOADER (iarch007 P1). Read a post-generation / post-verification checkpoint as
7165	    DATA ONLY for a --resume. Returns the payload dict, or None if the checkpoint is absent.
7166	
7167	    FAIL-LOUD §-1.3 enforcement: a checkpoint that smuggled a release verdict / "verified"/"released"
7168	    flag / D8 decision at the top level is a HARD ERROR (ValueError) — never silently loaded. This
7169	    makes "replay a stored verdict" structurally impossible: the only thing a resume can re-enter
7170	    with is verdict-free DATA, and every faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / D8)
7171	    RE-RUNS from scratch on that DATA. A malformed/corrupt JSON also raises (fail loud, never a
7172	    silent empty default)."""
7173	    _path = Path(run_dir) / checkpoint_name
7174	    if not _path.is_file():
7175	        return None
7176	    with _path.open(encoding="utf-8") as _handle:
7177	        _payload = json.load(_handle)  # raises on corrupt JSON -> fail loud
7178	    if not isinstance(_payload, dict):
7179	        raise ValueError(f"A12 checkpoint {checkpoint_name} is not a JSON object: {_path}")
7180	    _leaked = sorted(_A12_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS & set(_payload.keys()))
7181	    if _leaked:
7182	        raise ValueError(
7183	            f"A12 checkpoint {checkpoint_name} contains FORBIDDEN verdict key(s) {_leaked} — a "
7184	            "checkpoint stores DATA ONLY (§-1.3); a resume re-runs every gate and can NEVER replay "
7185	            f"a stored decision. Refusing to load {_path}."
7186	        )
7187	    return _payload
7188	
7189	
7190	# I-beatboth-008 (#1285) commit-2 build B: STORM-outline resume durability.
7191	# A --resume re-enters at the post-selection corpus_snapshot (or the post-fetch
7192	# fetch_snapshot), which is AFTER the STORM interview phase. The STORM block at
7193	# ~L5326 is guarded by `not _resume_active and not _resume_from_fetch`, so a resume
7194	# SKIPS the producer and `_storm_outline` would silently collapse to [] — the STORM
7195	# scaffold never fires on a resume (the bug). These two helpers persist the captured
7196	# outline on the fresh run and restore it on the resume so the scaffold survives.
7197	#
7198	# DATA ONLY: the payload carries section titles / order / keywords / descriptions
7199	# (STRUCTURE), never STORM-authored prose into verified_text and never any verdict.
7200	# A resume STILL re-runs every faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / D8);
7201	# this restores only the section scaffold the generator threads at ~L8768.
7202	_STORM_OUTLINE_CHECKPOINT = "storm_outline.json"
7203	_STORM_OUTLINE_STAGE = "post_interview"
7204	# Shared by BOTH persist + load so they can NEVER drift (a hardcoded `1` in each would be
7205	# the exact silent-drift class P1-3 guards against). Bump iff the SCAFFOLD field set below
7206	# changes incompatibly.
7207	_STORM_OUTLINE_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
7208	# The ONLY fields persisted per section (§-1.3 DATA-ONLY contract, I-beatboth-008 P1-2). These
7209	# are the STRUCTURAL scaffold fields the generator's `_build_storm_outline_section_plans`
7210	# consumes (it reads title + order; description + search_keywords are structural too). The
7211	# STORM-AUTHORED PROSE fields `evidence_summary` / `perspectives` (and any future verdict) are
7212	# DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED — they are never written, so a resume can never replay STORM prose as a
7213	# verified surface. The producer's fallback path also tacks on `storm_outline_degraded*` keys
7214	# (storm_interviews.py:1611) that are likewise dropped by this explicit allow-list projection.
7215	_STORM_OUTLINE_SCAFFOLD_FIELDS = ("title", "description", "search_keywords", "order")
7216	
7217	
7218	def _project_storm_outline_scaffold(section: "object") -> "dict | None":
7219	    """Project ONE StormOutlineSection (pydantic object OR dict OR fallback-dict) down to the
7220	    DATA-ONLY scaffold fields `_STORM_OUTLINE_SCAFFOLD_FIELDS`. Pick by NAME from whichever shape
7221	    (never a `dict(section)`-minus-keys, which would leak the fallback extras / any future field
7222	    / prose). Returns the projected dict, or None for an unrecognized shape (caller skips it,
7223	    never aborts)."""
7224	    if hasattr(section, "model_dump"):
7225	        _src = section.model_dump()
7226	    elif isinstance(section, dict):
7227	        _src = section
7228	    else:  # pragma: no cover - defensive; unexpected shape, skip not abort
7229	        return None
7230	    # Explicit allow-list: copy ONLY the 4 scaffold keys that are present; omit the rest.
7231	    # NEVER carries evidence_summary / perspectives / any verdict or prose.
7232	    return {_k: _src[_k] for _k in _STORM_OUTLINE_SCAFFOLD_FIELDS if _k in _src}
7233	
7234	
7235	def write_storm_outline_checkpoint(
7236	    run_dir: "Path", outline: "list"
7237	) -> "Path | None":
7238	    """Persist the captured STORM outline (post-interview) so a --resume can restore the
7239	    section scaffold the producer block skips. ATOMIC (temp + os.replace). Best-effort /
7240	    FAIL-OPEN: a write failure is logged by the caller and NEVER aborts the paid run
7241	    (the outline is a structural convenience, not a faithfulness artifact). Items are
7242	    `StormOutlineSection` pydantic objects; dicts are handled defensively. Each section is
7243	    projected to the DATA-ONLY scaffold fields ONLY (title/description/search_keywords/order);
7244	    `evidence_summary` / `perspectives` (STORM prose) and any verdict are NEVER persisted
7245	    (§-1.3, I-beatboth-008 P1-2). Returns the path written, or None on failure."""
7246	    try:
7247	        _sections = []
7248	        for _s in (outline or []):
7249	            _proj = _project_storm_outline_scaffold(_s)
7250	            if _proj is not None:
7251	                _sections.append(_proj)
7252	        payload = {
7253	            "schema_version": _STORM_OUTLINE_SCHEMA_VERSION,
7254	            "stage": _STORM_OUTLINE_STAGE,
7255	            "storm_outline": _sections,
7256	            # EXPLICIT invariant marker (§-1.3): a §-1.1 auditor sees on the artifact
7257	            # itself that this is STRUCTURE-ONLY DATA, no verdict, no STORM prose.
7258	            "faithfulness_invariant": (
7259	                "DATA ONLY; section titles/order/keywords/description (structure) — no "
7260	                "verdict, no STORM-authored prose (evidence_summary / perspectives are "
7261	                "NEVER persisted); a resume re-runs every faithfulness gate."
7262	            ),
7263	        }
7264	        _path = Path(run_dir) / _STORM_OUTLINE_CHECKPOINT
7265	        _tmp = _path.with_name(_path.name + ".tmp")
7266	        _tmp.write_text(
7267	            json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
7268	            encoding="utf-8",
7269	        )
7270	        os.replace(_tmp, _path)  # atomic publish
7271	        return _path
7272	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — FAIL-OPEN: checkpoint is best-effort, never a run blocker
7273	        return None
7274	
7275	
7276	def load_storm_outline_checkpoint(run_dir: "Path") -> "list | None":
7277	    """RESUME LOADER for the STORM outline. Returns a list of `StormOutlineSection`
7278	    objects if the checkpoint is PRESENT-and-valid, or None ONLY if the file is ABSENT.
7279	
7280	    FAIL-LOUD (mirrors the A12 loaders): a PRESENT file that is malformed RAISES — never a
7281	    silent empty default. "Malformed" means: corrupt JSON, not a JSON object, the wrong
7282	    `schema_version`, the wrong `stage`, a MISSING `storm_outline` key, a non-list
7283	    `storm_outline`, or a section dict that fails `StormOutlineSection(**d)`. This is the
7284	    P1-3 fix: the OLD `_payload.get("storm_outline", [])` returned [] for a present-but-
7285	    malformed file ({}, missing key, wrong schema/stage), silently reproducing the exact
7286	    resume STORM no-op this checkpoint exists to prevent. An EMPTY-but-valid outline
7287	    (`storm_outline: []`) legitimately returns [] (not a malformed file). STRUCTURE-ONLY:
7288	    no verdict is read."""
7289	    _path = Path(run_dir) / _STORM_OUTLINE_CHECKPOINT
7290	    if not _path.is_file():
7291	        return None  # ABSENT — the ONLY None case (fresh run / no snapshot)
7292	    from src.polaris_graph.agents.storm_interviews import StormOutlineSection
7293	    with _path.open(encoding="utf-8") as _handle:
7294	        _payload = json.load(_handle)  # raises on corrupt JSON -> fail loud
7295	    if not isinstance(_payload, dict):
7296	        raise ValueError(
7297	            f"STORM outline checkpoint is not a JSON object: {_path}"
7298	        )
7299	    _ver = _payload.get("schema_version")
7300	    if _ver != _STORM_OUTLINE_SCHEMA_VERSION:
7301	        raise ValueError(
7302	            f"STORM outline checkpoint schema_version {_ver!r} != expected "
7303	            f"{_STORM_OUTLINE_SCHEMA_VERSION!r} (present-but-malformed; refusing to silently "
7304	            f"drop the STORM scaffold): {_path}"
7305	        )
7306	    _stage = _payload.get("stage")
7307	    if _stage != _STORM_OUTLINE_STAGE:
7308	        raise ValueError(
7309	            f"STORM outline checkpoint stage {_stage!r} != expected "
7310	            f"{_STORM_OUTLINE_STAGE!r} (present-but-malformed): {_path}"
7311	        )
7312	    if "storm_outline" not in _payload:
7313	        raise ValueError(
7314	            f"STORM outline checkpoint is missing the required 'storm_outline' key "
7315	            f"(present-but-malformed; would have silently restored [] under the old "
7316	            f".get default): {_path}"
7317	        )
7318	    _sections = _payload["storm_outline"]
7319	    if not isinstance(_sections, list):
7320	        raise ValueError(
7321	            f"STORM outline checkpoint 'storm_outline' is not a list: {_path}"
7322	        )
7323	    # P1-3 (commit-2 iter-3) SECTION-LEVEL FAIL-LOUD: validate each section EXPLICITLY before
7324	    # construction — do NOT rely on `StormOutlineSection(**d)` to raise on a malformed section.
7325	    # The `normalize_field_names` model_validator (storm_interviews.py:380-383, mode="before")

7327	    # so `StormOutlineSection(**{})` constructs ONE BLANK-TITLE section instead of raising. A
7328	    # blank-title section then SILENTLY NO-OPS in `_build_storm_outline_section_plans` (blank
7329	    # titles are dropped) — the exact resume STORM no-op this checkpoint exists to prevent.
7330	    # An EMPTY-but-valid outline (`storm_outline: []`) skips this loop and legitimately yields [].
7331	    for _i, _d in enumerate(_sections):
7332	        if not isinstance(_d, dict):
7333	            raise ValueError(
7334	                f"STORM outline checkpoint section {_i} is not a dict "
7335	                f"(present-but-malformed; would not construct a usable scaffold section): "
7336	                f"{_d!r} in {_path}"
7337	            )
7338	        _title = _d.get("title")
7339	        if not isinstance(_title, str) or not _title.strip():
7340	            raise ValueError(
7341	                f"STORM outline checkpoint section {_i} has a missing/blank scaffold 'title' "
7342	                f"({_title!r}); a blank-title section silently no-ops the STORM scaffold builder "
7343	                f"(_build_storm_outline_section_plans drops blank titles) — refusing to silently "
7344	                f"restore a no-op scaffold (the P1-3 resume no-op this checkpoint prevents): "
7345	                f"{_path}"
7346	            )
7347	    # Each section is now a dict with a usable non-blank scaffold title. The persisted dicts
7348	    # carry ONLY the scaffold fields; the omitted required fields (evidence_summary /
7349	    # perspectives) fall back to the pydantic schema defaults ("" / []).
7350	    return [StormOutlineSection(**_d) for _d in _sections]

7353	def finalize_run_artifact(
7354	    run_dir: "Path | None",
7355	    summary: dict,
7356	    q: dict,
7357	    *,
7358	    timed_out: bool = False,
7359	    wall_clock_seconds: "float | None" = None,
7360	    log=None,
7361	) -> "str | None":
7362	    """B11 keystone — the Universal Artifact Finalizer. Guarantees EXACTLY ONE non-empty
7363	    human artifact exists in ``run_dir`` on EVERY exit path of a run.
7364	
7365	    Contract:
7366	      * If a NON-EMPTY ``report.md`` already exists (the success path, or any abort path
7367	        that wrote its own verdict body) -> NO-OP. NEVER overwrites a real report.md.
7368	      * Else -> write a NAMED backstop artifact (``report.md``) whose body matches the
7369	        already-computed terminal ``summary['status']`` (or the B20 timeout). The status
7370	        is READ, never decided here.
7371	
7372	    Idempotent + crash-safe: called from the run body's `finally` (catches exceptions /
7373	    pre-success aborts that wrote a manifest only) AND from the call-site timeout handler
7374	    (B20 — a hang the `finally` cannot reach). Whichever fires first wins; the second
7375	    no-ops on the now-present report.md.
7376	
7377	    MUST NEVER RAISE (it runs inside a `finally`): all IO is wrapped. Returns the artifact
7378	    kind written, or None if it no-op'd / could not write.
7379	
7380	    Faithfulness: PURE backstop. It asserts no claim and emits no findings — it can only
7381	    ADD a disclosure artifact, never relax a span / provenance / verdict check."""
7382	    def _emit_log(msg: str) -> None:
7383	        try:
7384	            if log is not None:
7385	                log(msg)
7386	            else:
7387	                logging.getLogger(__name__).info(msg)
7388	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — logging must never break the finalizer
7389	            pass
7390	
7391	    try:
7392	        if run_dir is None:
7393	            return None
7394	        report_path = Path(run_dir) / "report.md"
7395	        # NEVER overwrite a real (non-empty) report.md — the success path and the
7396	        # self-documenting abort bodies already wrote their artifact.
7397	        try:
7398	            if report_path.is_file() and report_path.stat().st_size > 0:
7399	                return None
7400	        except OSError:
7401	            # stat failed; fall through to a best-effort write (better an artifact
7402	            # than risk silence). A subsequent write replaces only an unreadable/empty file.
7403	            pass
7404	        status = summary.get("status") if isinstance(summary, dict) else None
7405	        error = summary.get("error") if isinstance(summary, dict) else None
7406	        body = build_finalizer_artifact_body(
7407	            research_question=q.get("question", "") if isinstance(q, dict) else "",
7408	            status=status,
7409	            error=error,
7410	            timed_out=timed_out,
7411	            wall_clock_seconds=wall_clock_seconds,
7412	        )
7413	        try:
7414	            Path(run_dir).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
7415	            report_path.write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
7416	        except OSError as exc:
7417	            _emit_log(f"[finalizer]   FAILED to write backstop artifact: {exc}")
7418	            return None
7419	        kind = artifact_kind_for_status(status, timed_out=timed_out)
7420	        _emit_log(
7421	            f"[finalizer]   B11 emitted backstop artifact kind={kind} "
7422	            f"status={status!r} timed_out={timed_out} (no report.md existed)"
7423	        )
7424	        return kind
7425	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a finally-callable finalizer must NEVER raise
7426	        _emit_log(f"[finalizer]   unexpected finalizer error (suppressed): {exc}")
7427	        return None

7430	def timeout_should_preserve_rendered_report(run_dir, timeout_summary) -> bool:
7431	    """WALLCLOCK-GUARD (I-deepfix-001): return True iff ``run_dir`` already holds a non-empty
7432	    report.md (mirror finalize_run_artifact's report.md guard). When True, recover the on-disk
7433	    manifest's truthful status into ``timeout_summary`` and the caller must NOT clobber manifest.json
7434	    with an error_unexpected timeout stamp. Pure/offline/never-raises. Faithfulness-neutral (outer
7435	    orchestration only — it reads report.md size + the existing manifest, writes nothing to the
7436	    faithfulness path)."""
7437	    try:
7438	        report_path = Path(run_dir) / "report.md"
7439	        rendered = report_path.is_file() and report_path.stat().st_size > 0
7440	    except OSError:
7441	        rendered = False
7442	    if not rendered:
7443	        return False
7444	    # ORDERING FIX (Codex iter-2 P1): a NON-EMPTY report.md is NOT proof of a GENUINE render.
7445	    # run_one_query's inner ``finally`` runs finalize_run_artifact BEFORE this outer preserve
7446	    # check, so on a genuine no-prior-report hang the inner finalizer's OWN non-empty TIMEOUT
7447	    # BACKSTOP report.md already sits on disk. That backstop (build_finalizer_artifact_body)
7448	    # stamps the machine-stable ``_FINALIZER_VERDICT_MARKER`` that a real findings report
7449	    # (``_reliability_md + final_report``) NEVER carries. If the on-disk report.md is that
7450	    # finalizer backstop, it is NOT a genuine render -> do NOT preserve, so the caller writes the
7451	    # labeled error/timeout manifest (a real hang is never silently mislabeled "already
7452	    # rendered"). Self-correcting on --resume: a later real render overwrites report.md and the
7453	    # marker is gone. Faithfulness-neutral (reads report.md text; writes nothing to any gate).
7454	    try:
7455	        _head = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")[:8192]
7456	    except OSError:
7457	        _head = ""
7458	    if _FINALIZER_VERDICT_MARKER in _head:
7459	        return False
7460	    try:
7461	        existing = json.loads((Path(run_dir) / "manifest.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
7462	        if isinstance(existing, dict) and isinstance(timeout_summary, dict):
7463	            existing_status = existing.get("status")
7464	            if existing_status is not None:
7465	                timeout_summary["status"] = existing_status
7466	            timeout_summary["manifest"] = existing
7467	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a missing/unreadable manifest still preserves the report
7468	        pass
7469	    return True

7472	def finalize_timeout_run_and_maybe_write_error_manifest(
7473	    run_dir, timeout_summary, q, *, wall_clock_seconds,
7474	) -> bool:
7475	    """B20 wall-clock TIMEOUT terminal-artifact writer (I-deepfix-001 ordering fix).
7476	
7477	    ONE seam shared by BOTH wall-clock handlers (the run_honest_sweep_r3 main sweep +
7478	    the run_gate_b paid path) so the finalize / preserve / error-manifest ORDERING lives
7479	    in a single unit-testable place. On a run-level wall-clock ``TimeoutError``:
7480	
7481	      1. Capture PRESERVE = a non-empty ``report.md`` ALREADY EXISTED (recovering its
7482	         on-disk manifest status into ``timeout_summary``) -- computed BEFORE the finalizer
7483	         runs.
7484	      2. ``finalize_run_artifact`` writes the NON-EMPTY timeout backstop ``report.md`` when
7485	         none existed, so a genuine hang STILL ships a human artifact (never silent).
7486	      3. PRESERVE True  -> a real ``report.md`` pre-existed; keep the truthful terminal
7487	                           manifest (do NOT clobber it with an ``error_unexpected`` stamp).
7488	         PRESERVE False -> a genuine no-report hang; write the labeled ``error_unexpected``
7489	                           timeout ``manifest.json``.
7490	
7491	    The ORDER is the fix: asking the preserve helper AFTER the finalizer would let the
7492	    finalizer's OWN backstop ``report.md`` fool it into returning True, suppressing the error
7493	    manifest for a real hang. Returns True iff a prior render was preserved.
7494	
7495	    Faithfulness-neutral -- outer orchestration only. Asserts no claim, emits no findings,
7496	    never touches strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding. Offline;
7497	    never raises into the caller."""
7498	    # ORDERING FIX (Codex P1): capture the PRESERVE decision from whether a non-empty report.md
7499	    # ALREADY EXISTED, BEFORE finalize_run_artifact writes its own non-empty backstop report.md.
7500	    # Deciding after the finalizer would let that backstop fool the helper into returning True,
7501	    # suppressing the error manifest for a genuine no-report hang.
7502	    _preserve = timeout_should_preserve_rendered_report(run_dir, timeout_summary)
7503	    try:
7504	        finalize_run_artifact(
7505	            run_dir, timeout_summary, q, timed_out=True, wall_clock_seconds=wall_clock_seconds,
7506	        )
7507	    except Exception as _fin_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never let the backstop break the run
7508	        print(f"[finalizer]   timeout-artifact write failed: {_fin_exc}")
7509	    if _preserve:
7510	        print(
7511	            "[finalizer]   wall-clock fired AFTER report.md rendered — preserving "
7512	            "terminal manifest (no error_unexpected clobber)"
7513	        )
7514	        return True
7515	    try:
7516	        _to_manifest = _base_manifest_envelope(
7517	            run_id="timeout", q=q, retrieval=None, run_cost=0.0,
7518	        )
7519	        _to_manifest["status"] = "error_unexpected"
7520	        _to_manifest["release_allowed"] = False
7521	        _to_manifest["run_wall_clock_timeout"] = True
7522	        _to_manifest["run_wall_clock_seconds"] = wall_clock_seconds
7523	        _to_manifest["error"] = (
7524	            timeout_summary.get("error") if isinstance(timeout_summary, dict) else None
7525	        )
7526	        # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) Fix 5 (Codex P1): route the timeout error-manifest through the
7527	        # single pre-write chokepoint so it carries the durable junk-deletion disclosure
7528	        # (deleted_chrome_nonsources / deleted_offtopic_sources) exactly like every OTHER
7529	        # manifest.json write site. Without this, a run that times out AFTER the seam wrote
7530	        # the durable junk_deletion_disclosure.json but BEFORE the report/success manifest
7531	        # would ship a timeout-abort manifest with the deletions silently omitted — a
7532	        # §-1.3.1 fail-loud breach (a deletion must be disclosed on EVERY manifest path).
7533	        # _attach_tool_utilization is fully fail-safe (no-op with no keys when the seam did
7534	        # not run this pass) and is itself wrapped by the enclosing try/except.
7535	        _to_manifest = _attach_tool_utilization(_to_manifest, Path(run_dir))
7536	        (Path(run_dir) / "manifest.json").write_text(
7537	            json.dumps(_to_manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
7538	            encoding="utf-8",
7539	        )
7540	        if isinstance(timeout_summary, dict):
7541	            timeout_summary["manifest"] = _to_manifest
7542	    except Exception as _man_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — manifest best-effort; artifact already shipped
7543	        print(f"[finalizer]   timeout-manifest write failed: {_man_exc}")
7544	    return False

7547	# Attribute / dict-key names the JUDGES lane (B12 credibility-side + B13 conflict-side) attaches
7548	# its PER-SOURCE / PER-PAIR labeled returns under. The JUDGES lane is NOT in this base (8002392e),
7549	# so the run-side glue detects these by NAME and tolerates their total absence. Listed as constants
7550	# so the JUDGES lane's eventual field name has ONE place to reconcile against.
7551	_CREDIBILITY_UNSCORED_ATTR = "credibility_unscored"
7552	_CONFLICT_UNSCORED_ATTR = "conflict_unscored"
7553	
7554	
7555	def _collect_judge_unscored_labels(multi, retrieval) -> dict:
7556	    """B12/B13 run-side glue (I-arch-005 #1257). DEFENSIVELY collect the JUDGES-lane PER-SOURCE
7557	    (``credibility_unscored``) and PER-PAIR (``conflict_unscored``) labeled returns from whichever
7558	    object the lane attaches them to (the generator result ``multi`` or the ``retrieval`` bundle),
7559	    tolerant of their TOTAL absence at this base (the lane is not integrated yet).
7560	
7561	    Detection is by ATTRIBUTE then dict-KEY name on each candidate carrier; a found value is
7562	    normalized to a list (a scalar/str is wrapped). Returns
7563	    ``{"credibility_unscored": [...], "conflict_unscored": [...]}`` with empty lists when absent.
7564	    Pure read-only; never raises into the run (the caller also guards)."""
7565	    def _read(carrier, name):
7566	        if carrier is None:
7567	            return None
7568	        val = getattr(carrier, name, None)
7569	        if val is None and isinstance(carrier, dict):
7570	            val = carrier.get(name)
7571	        return val
7572	
7573	    def _as_list(val):
7574	        if val is None:
7575	            return []
7576	        if isinstance(val, (list, tuple, set)):
7577	            return [v for v in val if v is not None]
7578	        return [val]
7579	
7580	    out = {_CREDIBILITY_UNSCORED_ATTR: [], _CONFLICT_UNSCORED_ATTR: []}
7581	    for carrier in (multi, retrieval):
7582	        for key in (_CREDIBILITY_UNSCORED_ATTR, _CONFLICT_UNSCORED_ATTR):
7583	            found = _as_list(_read(carrier, key))
7584	            if found:
7585	                out[key].extend(found)
7586	    return out

7590	# statuses that, under always-release, convert into a disclosed LABEL (the shipped report.md is
7591	# already strict_verify-clean span-grounded prose, so disclosing the run-level degradation and
7592	# SHIPPING keeps every per-claim faithfulness CHECK byte-unchanged). The binding 4-role D8 hold

7594	# coverage hold is NEVER converted — the faithfulness gate always wins.

7597	    # D8-binding path it is already demoted to advisory metadata; here we make it ship.

7600	        "(PT08/PT11/PT12). The binding faithfulness gates (strict_verify + 4-role D8) passed the "
7601	        "shipped prose; this advisory flag is DISCLOSED rather than withheld."
7602	    ),
7603	    # B19 — a FORCE-ENABLED discovery feature silently degraded to baseline (run-health /
7604	    # quantified-empty backstop). The corpus is the baseline corpus; disclose the degradation.
7605	    "abort_discovery_degraded": (
7606	        "discovery_degraded: a force-enabled discovery feature (STORM / agentic / quantified) did "
7607	        "not fire or produced no verified output, so the run used the baseline corpus. The "
7608	        "degradation is DISCLOSED; the shipped report is the strict_verify'd baseline run."
7609	    ),
7610	    # B19 — the strict-gate required-entity ledger raised (its honest 'Coverage gaps' disclosure
7611	    # could not be built). Disclose the ledger failure rather than hold the run.
7612	    "abort_required_entity_ledger_failed": (
7613	        "required_entity_ledger_failed: the required-entity coverage ledger could not be "
7614	        "built/rendered, so the explicit 'Coverage gaps' disclosure may be incomplete. The ledger "
7615	        "failure is DISCLOSED; the shipped report is otherwise strict_verify'd prose."
7616	    ),
7617	    # FIX-P0-B (I-arch-011 #1271, ALWAYS-RELEASE): a domain checklist left a topic marked
7618	    # `critical: true` APPLICABLE but UNCOVERED (the F11 completeness HOLD at the set-site ~9881,
7619	    # gated by PG_SWEEP_CRITICAL_COMPLETENESS_HOLD). The shipped report.md is already
7620	    # strict_verify-clean span-grounded prose on the sections that DID verify; the uncovered
7621	    # critical topic is a COMPLETENESS gap, not a faithfulness defect — so under always-release the
7622	    # verified body SHIPS with the uncovered topic(s) DISCLOSED as a coverage gap rather than a
7623	    # hard-abort. This generalizes the operator-locked "verifier LABELS, never HOLDS" reframe to the
7624	    # completeness gate: the per-claim faithfulness CHECK (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span
7625	    # grounding) is byte-unchanged — a real fabrication still HOLDS via the binding D8 path
7626	    # (`abort_four_role_release_held`, deliberately absent from this map). Domain-generic: any
7627	    # domain whose checklist carries a critical-but-uncovered topic converts identically. The
7628	    # SPECIFIC uncovered topic id(s) are appended to disclosed_gaps at the conversion site (so the
7629	    # gap is NAMED), on top of this generic disclosure header.
7630	    #
7631	    # REAL-RUN REACHABILITY (honest — read before trusting this entry):
7632	    #   * SEAM run (real benchmark path: run_gate_b sets PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1): the binding 4-role D8
7633	    #     seam OVERWRITES `summary_status` at ~10385 BEFORE this disposition runs (~11362), so the

7640	    #     fail-closes to four_role_held (body withheld) because no judge adjudicated. That is the
7641	    #     FAITHFUL outcome (a body cannot ship as released without real adjudication), NOT a bug.
7642	    # This entry is therefore the correct, forward-compatible, domain-generic disposition for the
7643	    # status IF it ever reaches b18 with D8 proof present; it never relaxes faithfulness. See the
7644	    # open_concerns in the FIX-P0-B build report + the always-release test module docstring.
7645	    "abort_critical_topic_uncovered": (

7648	        "cover it. The verified report body still ships; this completeness gap is DISCLOSED (LABEL "

7654	def b18_b19_disposition(
7655	    summary_status: str, *, always_release: bool
7656	) -> "tuple[str, str | None]":
7657	    """B18/B19 PURE disposition (I-arch-005 #1257). Decide the terminal status for a run-level
7658	    META HOLD under the always-release reframe, so a behavior test can exercise every status +
7659	    both ON/OFF without driving the pipeline.
7660	
7661	    Returns ``(new_status, disclosure_or_None)``:
7662	      * always-release ON + ``summary_status`` is a convertible META HOLD ->
7663	        ``("released_with_disclosed_gaps", <disclosure text>)`` (LABEL + SHIP).
7664	      * otherwise -> ``(summary_status, None)`` (UNCHANGED — OFF byte-identical, and a
7665	        non-convertible status such as the binding `abort_four_role_release_held` is untouched).
7666	
7667	    Faithfulness: PURE. It never converts the binding D8 hold (absent from the map) and never
7668	    touches a per-claim check — the shipped report.md is already strict_verify-clean."""
7669	    if always_release and summary_status in _B18_B19_CONVERTIBLE_HOLDS:
7670	        return "released_with_disclosed_gaps", _B18_B19_CONVERTIBLE_HOLDS[summary_status]
7671	    return summary_status, None

8062	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8063	# M-INT-0b — Pin capture on every sweep run (Phase E0)
8064	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8065	#
8066	# Wires `model_pin.capture_pin(...)` into every sweep run, so each
8067	# completed query writes a `model_pin.json` to its run_dir. Enables
8068	# `--replay-from-pin <path>` to apply a captured pin's runtime
8069	# configuration (env vars + model assignments) before re-running.
8070	#
8071	# Per FINAL_PLAN.md M-INT-0b acceptance:
8072	#   - Substrate IS imported (capture_pin, replay primitives) — see imports
8073	#   - Substrate IS invoked (this helper called from run_one_query)
8074	#   - Run-log evidence: model_pin.json written to run_dir; manifest.json
8075	#     references it
8076	#   - PG_CAPTURE_PIN=0 disables (rollback)
8077	#
8078	# Reproducibility/nondeterminism risk (FINAL_PLAN.md §F risk #3) is
8079	# mitigated by capturing the pin BEFORE Phase E1's parallel fetch /
8080	# cache / freshness integrations land. Every subsequent run is then
8081	# replayable.
8082	
8083	
8084	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8085	# M-INT-2 — Cache + cache-warming around sweep entry (Phase E1)
8086	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8087	#
8088	# Wires `cache_warming.warm_cache(...)` into the sweep so canonical
8089	# sources for each query can be pre-warmed before the live retrieval
8090	# runs. Idempotent: re-running the sweep on the same canonical URLs
8091	# skips the fetch (cache_hit_count>0) on the second pass.
8092	#
8093	# Acceptance bar:
8094	#   - Imported (warm_cache, CacheFetcher, RetrievalCacheStore)
8095	#   - Invoked (warm_cache called from main_async pre-sweep)
8096	#   - Run-log evidence: WarmingReport written to manifest path
8097	#   - PG_USE_CACHE_WARMING=0 disables (rollback)
8098	
8099	
8100	def _cache_db_path(out_root: Path) -> Path:
8101	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_RETRIEVAL_CACHE_DB_PATH")
8102	    if raw:
8103	        return Path(raw)
8104	    return out_root / "retrieval_cache.sqlite"
8105	
8106	
8107	def _warm_canonical_corpus(
8108	    workspace_id: str,
8109	    canonical_urls: list[str],
8110	    out_root: Path,
8111	) -> dict | None:
8112	    """Best-effort cache-warm. Returns the WarmingReport summary
8113	    dict, or None when disabled / no URLs / failure.
8114	
8115	    Failure logged but does NOT raise — telemetry/observability
8116	    must not gate the sweep.
8117	    """
8118	    if os.environ.get("PG_USE_CACHE_WARMING", "1") == "0":
8119	        return None
8120	    if not canonical_urls:
8121	        return None
8122	    try:
8123	        store = RetrievalCacheStore(_cache_db_path(out_root))
8124	
8125	        class _StubHttpFetcher:
8126	            """v1 noop fetcher: returns a minimal payload so the cache
8127	            entry exists. Real HTTP wiring comes in Phase F /
8128	            production. The substrate import + invocation is what
8129	            M-INT-2 demonstrates."""
8130	
8131	            def fetch(self, source_url: str) -> WarmFetchResult:
8132	                # Returning empty payload still creates the cache
8133	                # entry, demonstrating end-to-end wiring.
8134	                return WarmFetchResult(
8135	                    payload=b"",
8136	                    content_type="text/plain",
8137	                    fetch_status_code=200,
8138	                )
8139	
8140	        report = warm_cache(
8141	            store,
8142	            workspace_id,
8143	            canonical_urls,
8144	            _StubHttpFetcher(),
8145	            skip_existing=True,
8146	            on_fetcher_error="record",
8147	        )
8148	        return {
8149	            "fetched_count": report.fetched_count,
8150	            "skipped_count": report.skipped_count,
8151	            "errored_count": report.errored_count,
8152	            "started_at": report.started_at,
8153	            "finished_at": report.finished_at,
8154	        }
8155	    except Exception as exc:
8156	        print(f"[M-INT-2] WARN: cache warming failed: {exc}")
8157	        return None

8161	# M-INT-3 — Freshness detector + eviction (Phase E1)
8162	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8163	#
8164	# Wires `freshness_monitor.check_freshness(...)` +
8165	# FreshnessAlertStore into the sweep. For each canonical URL,
8166	# run a freshness check; if status is in evicting set
8167	# (superseded/retracted/EoC), evict from the cache.
8168	#
8169	# v1 stub detector: returns UNCHANGED for everything. Real
8170	# Crossref `update-policy` integration is Phase F.
8171	# Substrate import + invocation + SQL writes are demonstrated.
8172	
8173	
8174	def _freshness_db_path(out_root: Path) -> Path:
8175	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_FRESHNESS_DB_PATH")
8176	    if raw:
8177	        return Path(raw)
8178	    return out_root / "freshness_alerts.sqlite"
8179	
8180	
8181	def _check_corpus_freshness(
8182	    workspace_id: str,
8183	    canonical_urls: list[str],
8184	    out_root: Path,
8185	) -> dict | None:
8186	    """Best-effort freshness check + eviction. Returns a summary
8187	    dict with per-status counts, or None when disabled."""
8188	    if os.environ.get("PG_USE_FRESHNESS_DETECTOR", "1") == "0":
8189	        return None
8190	    if not canonical_urls:
8191	        return None
8192	    try:
8193	        alert_store = FreshnessAlertStore(_freshness_db_path(out_root))
8194	        cache_store = RetrievalCacheStore(_cache_db_path(out_root))
8195	
8196	        class _StubFreshnessDetector:
8197	            """v1 detector: returns UNCHANGED for every URL.
8198	            Real Crossref update-policy probe is Phase F.
8199	            Demonstrates substrate import + invocation +
8200	            FreshnessCheckResult shape."""
8201	
8202	            def detect(self, source_url: str) -> FreshnessCheckResult:
8203	                return FreshnessCheckResult(
8204	                    source_url=source_url,
8205	                    status=FreshnessStatus.UNCHANGED,
8206	                    details="stub detector v1 (real Crossref in Phase F)",
8207	                    new_canonical_url=None,
8208	                    fetched_status_code=200,
8209	                )
8210	
8211	        detector = _StubFreshnessDetector()
8212	        per_status: dict[str, int] = {
8213	            s.value: 0 for s in FreshnessStatus
8214	        }
8215	        evicted = 0
8216	        for url in canonical_urls:
8217	            try:
8218	                alert = check_freshness(
8219	                    workspace_id=workspace_id,
8220	                    source_url=url,
8221	                    detector=detector,
8222	                    store=alert_store,
8223	                    cache=cache_store,
8224	                )
8225	                per_status[alert.status] = per_status.get(alert.status, 0) + 1
8226	                if alert.evicted_cache_key is not None:
8227	                    evicted += 1
8228	            except Exception as exc:
8229	                print(f"[M-INT-3] WARN: freshness check failed for "
8230	                      f"{url}: {exc}")
8231	        return {
8232	            "per_status": per_status,
8233	            "evicted_count": evicted,
8234	            "total_checked": len(canonical_urls),
8235	        }
8236	    except Exception as exc:
8237	        print(f"[M-INT-3] WARN: freshness path failed: {exc}")
8238	        return None

8352	def _route_query_to_domain(
8353	    classification,
8354	    *,
8355	    requested_domain: str | None = None,
8356	) -> dict | None:
8357	    """Best-effort domain routing for the LLM scope classification.
8358	    Telemetry only — does NOT gate retrieval. PG_USE_DOMAIN_ROUTER=0
8359	    disables. Per LAW II, internal failure returns None (does not raise).
8360	
8361	    Codex round-1 MEDIUM fix (v2): when result.template is None
8362	    (UNKNOWN_DOMAIN, REJECTED_*, MISSING_ADAPTERS), the routing
8363	    summary's `domain` was None — the original LLM-asserted domain
8364	    tag was lost. v2 surfaces the original `requested_domain`
8365	    alongside `domain` so unknown-domain telemetry preserves
8366	    "user asked for X, registry doesn't know it" signal.
8367	    """
8368	    if os.environ.get("PG_USE_DOMAIN_ROUTER", "0") == "0":
8369	        return None

8371	    if requested_domain is None and hasattr(classification, "domain"):
8372	        requested_domain = classification.domain

8374	        registry = _build_domain_router_registry()
8375	        adapters = _build_domain_router_adapters()
8376	        result = route_to_domain(classification, registry, adapters)
8377	        return {
8378	            "outcome": result.outcome.value,
8379	            "domain": (
8380	                result.template.domain_id if result.template else None
8381	            ),

8396	def _check_audit_run_quota() -> dict | None:
8397	    """Best-effort billing quota check + consume for one audit run.
8398	    Returns a summary dict or None when disabled / no org configured.
8399	
8400	    Per LAW II — internal failure returns None (does not raise).
8401	    QuotaExceededError is caught and returned as a structured
8402	    summary with `consumed=False, exceeded=True` so the caller
8403	    can decide whether to abort the run with a quota status.
8404	
8405	    PG_USE_BILLING_QUOTA=0 disables (default 0 in v1).
8406	    PG_BILLING_ORG_ID specifies which org to charge.
8407	    """
8408	    if os.environ.get("PG_USE_BILLING_QUOTA", "0") == "0":
8409	        return None
8410	    org_id = os.environ.get("PG_BILLING_ORG_ID", "").strip()
8411	    if not org_id:
8412	        # No org configured — best-effort, don't gate sweep without
8413	        # an explicit assignment.
8414	        return None
8415	    try:
8416	        db_path = Path(os.environ.get(
8417	            "PG_BILLING_QUOTA_DB_PATH",
8418	            str(Path("state") / "billing_quota.sqlite"),
8419	        ))
8420	        store = BillingQuotaStore(db_path)
8421	        try:
8422	            event = store.consume(
8423	                org_id=org_id,
8424	                kind=QuotaEventKind.AUDIT_RUN_ENQUEUED,
8425	            )
8426	            check = store.check_quota(
8427	                org_id=org_id,
8428	                kind=QuotaEventKind.AUDIT_RUN_ENQUEUED,
8429	            )
8430	            return {
8431	                "consumed": True,
8432	                "exceeded": False,
8433	                "org_id": org_id,
8434	                "event_id": event.event_id,
8435	                "used": check.used,
8436	                "cap": check.cap,
8437	                "remaining": check.remaining,
8438	            }
8439	        except QuotaExceededError as exc:
8440	            return {
8441	                "consumed": False,
8442	                "exceeded": True,
8443	                "org_id": org_id,
8444	                "reason": str(exc),
8445	            }
8446	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
8447	        print(f"[M-INT-7] WARN: billing quota path failed: {exc}")
8448	        return None

8473	def _record_operator_review_item(
8474	    *,
8475	    run_dir: Path,
8476	    query: str,
8477	    verdict: InductorVerdict,
8478	) -> None:
8479	    """Append an abstaining inductor verdict to the run's
8480	    operator-review queue (one JSONL per sweep run). Called only
8481	    when verdict.decision == 'abstain' — the operator-review
8482	    queue is exactly the set of cases the inductor declined to
8483	    auto-induce."""
8484	    queue_path = run_dir / "operator_review_queue.jsonl"
8485	    item = {

8494	        f.write(json.dumps(item, sort_keys=True) + "\n")

8508	    gate retrieval. PG_USE_AUTO_INDUCTION=0 disables.

8525	            "induced_slug": (

8527	                if verdict.induced_contract is not None else None

8529	        }

8583	# I-ready-005 (#1076) Codex iter-1 P1: per-feature firing telemetry must land on EVERY manifest write
8584	# (success AND all abort/budget/error paths), not just success — else a forced-ON run that aborts early
8585	# (e.g. abort_no_sources, AFTER the STORM block) leaves the operator unable to prove STORM/agentic fired.
8586	# run_one_query publishes its (live, mutated-in-place) telemetry dicts into this ContextVar at the top;
8587	# _attach_tool_utilization — the single hook called before every manifest.json write — stamps them.
8588	# ContextVar (not a module global) so concurrent async run_one_query tasks don't stomp each other.

8594	def _attach_junk_deletion_disclosure(manifest: dict, run_dir: "Path | None") -> dict:
8595	    """I-deepfix-003 (#1374) Fix 5: attach the split junk-deletion disclosure
8596	    (``deleted_chrome_nonsources`` + ``deleted_offtopic_sources``) to ``manifest`` from the
8597	    DURABLE ``run_dir/junk_deletion_disclosure.json`` the seam wrote. Called from
8598	    ``_attach_tool_utilization`` so EVERY manifest write path (success + every abort/error site)
8599	    carries it — a deletion is never silent (§-1.3.1 fail-loud), and the deleted_* keys land on
8600	    abort manifests too.
8601	
8602	    Keys are attached ONLY when the durable file EXISTS (i.e. the seam actually ran this pass):
8603	    a pre-seam abort leaves the manifest byte-identical (no keys), so this never changes an
8604	    abort shape for a run that never reached the gate. ``deleted_offtopic_sources`` matches ANY
8605	    ``confirmed_offtopic*`` reason (Fix 1 emits ``confirmed_offtopic_subject``; the legacy path
8606	    emits ``confirmed_offtopic``) — the prior ``== "confirmed_offtopic"`` filter silently MISSED
8607	    the Fix-1 reason. FAIL-OPEN: any error leaves the manifest untouched (never abort a run for
8608	    a disclosure read)."""
8609	    try:
8610	        if run_dir is None:
8611	            return manifest
8612	        _p = Path(run_dir) / "junk_deletion_disclosure.json"
8613	        if not _p.is_file():
8614	            return manifest  # gate did not run this pass — byte-identical (no keys)
8615	        _payload = json.loads(_p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
8616	        _recs = _payload.get("deleted", []) if isinstance(_payload, dict) else []
8617	        if not isinstance(_recs, list):
8618	            _recs = []
8619	        manifest["deleted_chrome_nonsources"] = [
8620	            r for r in _recs
8621	            if isinstance(r, dict)
8622	            and str(r.get("deletion_reason", "")).startswith("content_integrity_junk")
8623	        ]
8624	        manifest["deleted_offtopic_sources"] = [
8625	            r for r in _recs
8626	            if isinstance(r, dict)
8627	            and str(r.get("deletion_reason", "")).startswith("confirmed_offtopic")
8628	        ]
8629	    except Exception as _jd_disc_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — disclosure attach must never abort a run
8630	        print(f"[junk-deletion-gate] disclosure attach skipped: {_jd_disc_exc}")
8631	    return manifest
8632

8634	def _attach_tool_utilization(manifest: dict, run_dir: Path) -> dict:
8635	    """I-meta-007b (#meta-007) P1: attach the per-run tool-utilization summary
8636	    to ``manifest`` (and write ``run_dir/tool_summary.json``) immediately before
8637	    EVERY ``manifest.json`` write — success AND all abort/error paths.
8638	
8639	    Thin fail-safe wrapper around
8640	    :func:`src.polaris_graph.telemetry.tool_tracer.attach_tool_utilization`.
8641	    Pure no-op when PG_ENABLE_TOOL_TRACKER selects OFF (manifest unchanged, no
8642	    file written), so OFF-mode manifest.json stays byte-identical. Any telemetry
8643	    or import error is swallowed + logged so it can never abort the run.
8644	
8645	    I-ready-005 (#1076): ALSO stamps the per-feature firing telemetry (STORM / agentic) from
8646	    ``_FEATURE_TELEMETRY_CTX`` so EVERY manifest write path carries it. Additive, fail-safe.
8647	    """
8648	    # I-ready-005 (#1076): stamp the per-feature firing telemetry on EVERY manifest path. Best-effort.
8649	    _feat = _FEATURE_TELEMETRY_CTX.get()
8650	    if _feat:
8651	        for _k, _v in _feat.items():
8652	            manifest[_k] = _v
8653	    # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) Fix 5: attach the durable junk-deletion disclosure on EVERY manifest
8654	    # write path (this hook is the single pre-write chokepoint). Fail-open; no-op (no keys) when
8655	    # the seam did not run this pass.
8656	    _attach_junk_deletion_disclosure(manifest, run_dir)
8657	    try:
8658	        from src.polaris_graph.telemetry.tool_tracer import (
8659	            attach_tool_utilization as _attach,
8660	        )
8661	        return _attach(manifest, run_dir)
8662	    except Exception as _tt_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must not abort the run
8663	        print(f"[tool_tracker] utilization attach skipped: {_tt_exc}")
8664	        return manifest

8667	def write_terminal_error_manifest(
8668	    run_dir: "Path | None",
8669	    q: dict,
8670	    run_id: "str | None",
8671	    exc: BaseException,
8672	    phase: str,
8673	) -> "dict | None":
8674	    """BUG-21 (#1262): write a TERMINAL manifest.json with ``status=error_<phase>`` on an
8675	    otherwise-uncaught exception, so NO catchable failure leaves run_status.json frozen at an
8676	    intermediate stage with no terminal artifact (the drb_76 silent-death class).
8677	
8678	    The big outer ``try`` inside ``run_one_query`` already writes an error manifest for any failure
8679	    INSIDE it; this helper covers the ENTRY-SETUP region BEFORE that try (corrupt-snapshot resume
8680	    load, a reasoning-collector / telemetry / import failure), which previously escaped with no
8681	    manifest. ``phase`` is the last heartbeat stage reached so the human sees WHERE it died. The
8682	    record carries the exception TYPE + message; status is ``error_<phase>`` (an ``error_*`` value,
8683	    consistent with the unified taxonomy's ``error_unexpected`` family — the EXISTING success /
8684	    abort / error_unexpected statuses are untouched and byte-identical).
8685	
8686	    Best-effort and never raises: a write failure here must not mask the original exception, and
8687	    this is observability / crash-safety ONLY — it touches no faithfulness gate, span, or verdict.
8688	    Returns the written manifest dict (or None when no run_dir is available)."""
8689	    if run_dir is None:
8690	        return None
8691	    try:
8692	        _safe_phase = "".join(
8693	            c if (c.isalnum() or c == "_") else "_" for c in (phase or "unknown")
8694	        ) or "unknown"
8695	        run_cost = current_run_cost()
8696	        manifest = {
8697	            "run_id": run_id or "",
8698	            "slug": q.get("slug", ""),
8699	            "domain": q.get("domain", ""),
8700	            "question": q.get("question", ""),
8701	            "status": f"error_{_safe_phase}",
8702	            "error": str(exc)[:500],
8703	            "error_type": type(exc).__name__,
8704	            "phase": phase or "unknown",
8705	            "cost_usd": run_cost,
8706	            "budget_cap_usd": get_max_cost_per_run(),
8707	        }
8708	        manifest = augment_v6_manifest(
8709	            manifest,
8710	            external_run_id=q.get("external_run_id"),
8711	            decision_id=q.get("decision_id"),
8712	            query_slug=q.get("slug"),
8713	        )
8714	        manifest = _attach_tool_utilization(manifest, run_dir)
8715	        (run_dir / "manifest.json").write_text(
8716	            json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
8717	            encoding="utf-8",
8718	        )
8719	        return manifest
8720	    except Exception as _terminal_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
8721	        try:
8722	            logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
8723	                "terminal error-manifest write failed (phase=%s): %s", phase, _terminal_exc
8724	            )
8725	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
8726	            pass
8727	        return None

8730	def _abort_if_cancelled(
8731	    q: dict,
8732	    run_dir: Path,
8733	    run_id: str,
8734	    summary: dict,
8735	    log_fn,
8736	) -> bool:
8737	    """I-rdy-011 (#507): cooperative cancel checkpoint for a v6 run.
8738	
8739	    If a cancellation was requested for this v6 run, write a terminal
8740	    `cancelled` manifest, emit the SSE terminal event, mark the summary, and
8741	    return True so run_one_query aborts early at this stage boundary. Returns
8742	    False for non-v6 runs or when no cancel is pending.
8743	
8744	    Best-effort: a run_store lookup failure returns False — a healthy run is
8745	    never aborted on a transient backend hiccup.
8746	    """
8747	    ext = q.get("external_run_id")
8748	    if not (q.get("v6_mode") and ext):
8749	        return False
8750	    try:
8751	        from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
8752	
8753	        if not run_store.is_cancel_requested(ext):
8754	            return False
8755	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — cancel-check failure must not abort a healthy run
8756	        return False
8757	    log_fn("[cancel]      cancellation requested — aborting run cooperatively")
8758	    manifest = {
8759	        "run_id": run_id,
8760	        "slug": q.get("slug", ""),
8761	        "domain": q.get("domain", ""),
8762	        "question": q.get("question", ""),
8763	        "status": "cancelled",
8764	    }
8765	    manifest = _attach_tool_utilization(manifest, run_dir)
8766	    (run_dir / "manifest.json").write_text(
8767	        json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
8768	    )
8769	    emit_event(ext, "run.completed", {"status": "cancelled"})
8770	    summary["status"] = "cancelled"
8771	    return True

8774	def _normalize_quantified_telemetry(telemetry: dict) -> dict:

8787	def quantified_silent_no_op_canary(telemetry: "dict | None") -> "dict | None":
8788	    """I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3b LANE C: a machine-assertable CANARY for the Phase-7 quantified
8789	    SILENT no-op.
8790	
8791	    The quantified block intentionally NEVER aborts the run (broad ``except`` in run_one_query), so
8792	    a block that RAN but produced no verified quantified sentence used to surface ONLY as a buried
8793	    log line + a nested ``manifest.quantified_analysis.fired=False`` field — easy for a behavioral
8794	    preflight / audit to miss. This returns a structured signal dict the caller stamps as the
8795	    top-level ``manifest.quantified_silent_no_op`` key, so the no-op is a single explicit signal a
8796	    test can assert on (the fail-LOUD the no-downgrade directive requires).
8797	
8798	    Returns None when the block did NOT run (``telemetry`` absent) OR DID fire (``fired`` truthy) —
8799	    i.e. no canary needed. PURE; NON-gating (it never changes run status; the force-on strict-gate
8800	    abort that DOES hold a force-enabled empty differentiator lives separately, keyed on the same
8801	    ``fired`` boolean)."""
8802	    if not isinstance(telemetry, dict):
8803	        return None
8804	    if telemetry.get("fired"):
8805	        return None
8806	    return {
8807	        "feature": "quantified_analysis",
8808	        "fired": False,
8809	        "silent_no_op": True,
8810	        "firing_status": (
8811	            telemetry.get("firing_status")
8812	            or telemetry.get("quantified_status")
8813	            or "unknown"
8814	        ),
8815	        "verified_sentences": int(telemetry.get("verified_sentences", 0) or 0),
8816	    }

8829	    """Raised by ``assert_no_quantified_silent_no_op`` ONLY in a gating (preflight / test)
8830	    context when the quantified differentiator RAN but produced no verified output. It is NEVER
8831	    raised on a real release run: production stamps the manifest canary + logs at ERROR and keeps
8832	    going (the existing ``_quantified_readiness_failed`` strict+force gate is the separate, intended
8833	    production HOLD). This exception exists so the no-op is caught BEFORE a real run by the
8834	    behavioral preflight / unit test."""

8837	def assert_no_quantified_silent_no_op(
8838	    canary: "dict | None", *, gating: bool,
8839	) -> None:
8840	    """Gating assertion over the quantified silent-no-op canary (the dict from
8841	    ``quantified_silent_no_op_canary`` — or None when the block fired / never ran).
8842	
8843	    ``gating=True`` (preflight / test context): RAISE ``QuantifiedSilentNoOpError`` when ``canary``
8844	    signals a no-op, so a silent no-op is caught LOUDLY before a real run.
8845	    ``gating=False`` (the production default): ADVISORY — return None, never raise, so a real
8846	    release run is NOT aborted on a P2-class differentiator gap (operator: never hold the report).
8847	
8848	    PURE; no I/O. Idempotent. ``canary is None`` is always a clean pass (no no-op)."""
8849	    if canary is not None and gating:
8850	        firing_status = (
8851	            canary.get("firing_status", "unknown")
8852	            if isinstance(canary, dict) else "unknown"
8853	        )
8854	        raise QuantifiedSilentNoOpError(
8855	            f"{QUANTIFIED_SILENT_NO_OP_MESSAGE} (firing_status={firing_status})"
8856	        )
8857	    return None

8860	def _judge_calls_and_errors_from_telemetry(
8861	    base_tel: dict, now_tel: dict,
8862	) -> tuple[int, int]:
8863	    """FX-09 (I-ready-017): (judge_calls, judge_errors) for THIS run, as the delta of the
8864	    process-lifetime entailment-judge telemetry counters.
8865	
8866	    denominator = actual judge invocations this run (calls delta); numerator = judge_error
8867	    delta (counts errors even on KEPT sentences that failed open). Pure + reentrant: the
8868	    sweep snapshots `base` at the run boundary and never resets the global counters.
8869	    """
8870	    calls = int(now_tel.get("calls", 0)) - int(base_tel.get("calls", 0))
8871	    errors = int(now_tel.get("judge_error", 0)) - int(base_tel.get("judge_error", 0))
8872	    return calls, errors

8879	    magic number; single source so the EARLY abort-attribution check and the canonical

8884	def judge_error_degraded_from_telemetry(run_judge_tel: "dict | None") -> "tuple[bool, float, int, int]":
8885	    """ITEM 3 (I-arch-007 death-forensic): PURE abort-attribution predicate — is the
8886	    binding entailment verifier so degraded that ``judge_error_rate >
8887	    PG_MAX_JUDGE_ERROR_RATE``? Mirrors the canonical late computation
8888	    (``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` verification stage: zero-base delta of the per-run,
8889	    concurrency-isolated telemetry counter) so the EARLY gap-abort relabel and the late
8890	    always-release LABEL path agree byte-for-byte on the rate.
8891	
8892	    Returns ``(degraded, rate, judge_calls, judge_errors)``. ``run_judge_tel`` is the live
8893	    per-run ``{calls, judge_error}`` dict bound by ``begin_run_judge_telemetry()`` at the
8894	    run boundary; by the time the verified-section floor is evaluated, generation +
8895	    per-sentence verification have completed, so this counter is already FINAL — reading it
8896	    here is a pure observation with no side effect and no double-count (zero base, same as
8897	    the late read).
8898	
8899	    When telemetry is unavailable (``None`` — the rare import-failure path), this returns
8900	    ``degraded=False``: the early relabel is INERT and today's gap-abort label stands
8901	    unchanged. The late canonical path's reason-grep fallback (which needs ``verif_details``,
8902	    not yet built at the floor gate) remains the authority in that degraded-telemetry case.
8903	    This is a LABEL-only attribution helper; it touches no faithfulness gate, threshold, or
8904	    cited-evidence set."""
8905	    if run_judge_tel is None:
8906	        return (False, 0.0, 0, 0)
8907	    judge_calls, judge_errors = _judge_calls_and_errors_from_telemetry(
8908	        {"calls": 0, "judge_error": 0}, run_judge_tel,
8909	    )
8910	    rate = (judge_errors / judge_calls) if judge_calls else 0.0
8911	    return (rate > max_judge_error_rate(), rate, judge_calls, judge_errors)

8915	    *, has_verified_sections: bool, excessive_gap: bool, judge_degraded: bool,
8916	) -> str:
8917	    """ITEM 3 (I-arch-007 death-forensic): PURE 3-way abort-cause selector for the
8918	    gap/empty terminal block. This mirrors the EXACT inline priority in ``run_one_query``
8919	    so the cause-attribution ordering is unit-testable without driving the giant run.
8920	
8921	    Precondition: the caller only enters this block when ``not has_verified_sections OR
8922	    excessive_gap`` (the verified-section floor gate). Priority:
8923	      1. ``judge_degraded`` → ``abort_verifier_degraded`` — the binding verifier bricked;
8924	         the verified-section shortfall is a VERIFIER outage, NOT a coverage gap. This wins
8925	         so the operator is NOT told to "widen retrieval" (the exactly-wrong remedy).
8926	      2. ``excessive_gap`` → ``abort_excessive_gap`` — a genuine coverage shortfall with a
8927	         HEALTHY verifier.

8932	    if judge_degraded:
8933	        return "abort_verifier_degraded"
8934	    if excessive_gap:
8935	        return "abort_excessive_gap"
8936	    return "abort_no_verified_sections"

9029	def _gate_load_or_compile_artifact(prompt, run_dir, *, run_id, log):
9030	    """Acquire the pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact` for the S2 retrieval seam.
9031	
9032	    Priority (all OFFLINE-safe, no live retrieval):
9033	      1. a pre-pinned ``<run_dir>/planning_gate_artifact.json`` (the canonical
9034	         location ``run_gate_e2e`` writes; the monitored-live-job path) — loaded
9035	         verbatim so its pinned ``contract_sha256`` is honored, NOT recomputed;
9036	      2. else compile it via ``research_planning_gate.run_research_planning_gate``
9037	         (mode="autonomous", the small policy compiler LLM — fast, spend-bounded)
9038	         and pin it to that same path.
9039	
9040	    Returns the ``PlanningGateArtifact`` or ``None`` on any failure (fail-open:
9041	    the seam then keeps the champion path, byte-identical). This is called ONLY
9042	    under ``PG_GATE`` — with the flag unset it never runs.
9043	    """
9044	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import artifact_from_dict
9045	
9046	    art_path = run_dir / "planning_gate_artifact.json"
9047	
9048	    # (1) a pre-pinned artifact — the canonical run_gate_e2e location.
9049	    if art_path.exists():
9050	        try:
9051	            artifact = artifact_from_dict(json.loads(art_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
9052	            log(f"[planning_gate] S2 loaded pinned artifact "
9053	                f"contract_sha256={(artifact.contract_sha256 or '')[:12]} "
9054	                f"(hard_terms={len(artifact.contract.hard_terms())})")
9055	            return artifact
9056	        except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open to compile / champion
9057	            log(f"[planning_gate] S2 pinned artifact unreadable, will compile: {_exc}")
9058	
9059	    # (2) compile + pin. Autonomous mode NEVER blocks (no input channel here).
9060	    # FIX 2(c): reaching this branch means the PINNED artifact the upstream entrypoint
9061	    # wrote (run_gate_e2e / monitored-live-job) was ABSENT at the task-level run_dir the
9062	    # seam reads — the exact KEYSTONE class of bug (a silently-swapped, recompiled contract
9063	    # that steered a full run for a day). Fail-LOUD: WARNING log + a durable manifest stamp
9064	    # (via _FEATURE_TELEMETRY_CTX -> _attach_tool_utilization stamps EVERY manifest path).
9065	    # This runs ONLY under PG_GATE (the whole helper is behind _pg_gate_enabled()), so the
9066	    # PG_GATE-OFF path is byte-identical (this code is never reached there).
9067	    _stamp_gate_recompiled_at_seam(log)
9068	    try:
9069	        import asyncio as _asyncio
9070	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planning_gate import (
9071	            run_research_planning_gate,
9072	        )
9073	
9074	        def _compile():
9075	            return _asyncio.run(
9076	                run_research_planning_gate(prompt, mode="autonomous", run_id=run_id)
9077	            )
9078	
9079	        import concurrent.futures as _futures
9080	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _pool:
9081	            gate = _pool.submit(_compile).result()
9082	        artifact = gate.artifact
9083	        try:
9084	            art_path.write_text(
9085	                json.dumps(artifact.to_dict(), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
9086	                encoding="utf-8",
9087	            )
9088	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — pinning is best-effort; the artifact still steers
9089	            pass
9090	        log(f"[planning_gate] S2 compiled + pinned artifact "
9091	            f"contract_sha256={(artifact.contract_sha256 or '')[:12]} "
9092	            f"(hard_terms={len(artifact.contract.hard_terms())})")
9093	        return artifact
9094	    except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open to champion path
9095	        log(f"[planning_gate] S2 artifact compile unavailable, champion path: {_exc}")
9096	        return None

9099	def _stamp_gate_recompiled_at_seam(log) -> None:
9100	    """FIX 2(c): record that the S2 seam had to RECOMPILE the gate contract because the
9101	    pinned artifact was absent at the task-level run_dir.
9102	
9103	    Emits a WARNING-level line and stamps ``manifest['gate_contract_recompiled_at_seam']=True``
9104	    on EVERY manifest write path by merging into the per-Task ``_FEATURE_TELEMETRY_CTX``
9105	    (the same channel ``_attach_tool_utilization`` drains before every manifest.json write).
9106	    Best-effort + fail-open: a telemetry fault must never abort the run. Called ONLY from the
9107	    recompile branch (behind PG_GATE), so the OFF path is byte-identical."""
9108	    try:
9109	        log("[planning_gate] WARNING: RECOMPILED AT SEAM (pinned artifact absent) — the "
9110	            "upstream-pinned contract did NOT reach the sweep run_dir; the seam recompiled a "
9111	            "fresh contract. This may silently swap the contract that steers the run.")
9112	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
9113	        pass
9114	    try:
9115	        _feat = _FEATURE_TELEMETRY_CTX.get()
9116	        if _feat is None:
9117	            _feat = {}
9118	            _FEATURE_TELEMETRY_CTX.set(_feat)
9119	        _feat["gate_contract_recompiled_at_seam"] = True
9120	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must not abort the run
9121	        pass
